action.reasoning: Observation: Latest test_enhance.py confirms MetricGAN+ with -25 LUFS still best on val (PESQ=2.8620) vs ConvTasNet (2.0073) and hybrid (2.3728); current test best remains 3.137. train.py implements ConvTasNet but underperforms significantly after 10+ epochs (train loss improved but val PESQ low). predict.py correctly implements MetricGAN+ offline load + loudness norm from best_loudness.txt + /tmp/enhanced per contract. Analysis: MetricGAN+ is task-specific (directly optimizes PESQ on VoiceBank+DEMAND), explaining why from-scratch ConvTasNet (small arch, SISDR+MRSTFT) struggles to catch up quickly; postproc (loudness, hybrid, iterative) saturated with flat val curve. With 3.4h/10 iters left, further ConvTasNet scaling (larger model, more epochs, fixed val loop) is viable but risky without quick wins. Decision: Submit current strongest config (metricgan_checkpoint + predict.py with -25 norm) immediately to lock in best score and measure eval time before iterating on improved training (e.g. larger ConvTasNet, HDemucs, better scheduler/loss balance, loudness in val).
